name: Foundation (Standard Rules)
tagline: Classic table tennis — the baseline for every variant.
rules: Players: 2 or 4.
Scoring: Games to 11 points; win by 2 (deuce at 10–10).
Let: Serve hits the net but lands correctly — replay with no penalty.
Table boundaries: Top surface and edges are in; vertical sides are out.
Free hand: Touching the playing surface with your non-paddle hand while the ball is in play loses the point.
Service toss: Ball on open palm, tossed at least 6 inches straight up, struck on the way down (unless a variant allows direct serve).

name: Volley-Pong
tagline: High-flying teamwork — pass, volley, and position.
rules: Players: 4 or 6 (2v2 or 3v3).
Serve: Direct serve over the net (no bounce on server's side); must land on the diagonal receiver's side.
Mandatory passing: After the ball hits the receiver's side, that player cannot return over the net — they must pop the ball up to a teammate.
The volley: Teammates hit passes out of the air (no table bounce required on passes) and may pass again or strike back over the net.
Consecutive hits: One player cannot hit the ball twice in a row.
Movement: Players may cross the net line to save a pass but cannot strike over the net from the opponent's side.
Play-on: Once struck, the ball stays in play even if it was clearly going out.

name: Table-Tennis Tennis
tagline: Fast, aggressive play — volleys and single bounces like lawn tennis.
rules: Players: 2, 4, or 6.
Serve: Direct serve over the net (no bounce on server's side).
Volley: Strike the ball out of the air before it bounces on your side.
Bounce: If you don't volley, the ball may bounce only once on your side before you return it.
Play-on: If you touch or hit the ball out of the air, it stays live — you cannot claim "out" after contact.

name: Synchronized Solos (Solos in Duos)
tagline: Two 1v1 matches on one table — pure chaos.
rules: Players: 4 (two simultaneous 1v1 games).
Gameplay: Standard ping pong rules with two balls in play at once.
Boundaries — choose before play:
Parallel play: Left-side players only vs left-side; right-side only vs right-side.
Crossfire: Players play diagonally against each other.
Faults: Ball in your partner's zone or wrong opponent's side — your 1v1 match loses a point.

name: The Gauntlet (Trios)
tagline: Structured 3v3 rotation — ultimate focus on turn order.
rules: Players: 6 (3v3). Team A: P1, P2, P3. Team B: EP1, EP2, EP3.
Rotation: Ball must be struck in order P1 → EP1 → P2 → EP2 → P3 → EP3 → P1… Hitting out of turn = point for opponents.
Serve: P1 and P2 handle standard 2-serve rotations; when P2 serves to EP2, the next hit must be P3 to maintain rotation.
Clutch serving: At game point, deuce, or advantage, P3 serves to EP3; EP3 must hit to P1, resetting the loop.

name: Zonal Duos (No-Move Duos)
tagline: Territorial doubles — no alternating hits required.
rules: Players: 4 (2v2).
Gameplay: Standard ping pong except players do not alternate hits.
Territory: Each player owns their half of the table (divided by the center line). Ball on your half — you hit it; on partner's half — they hit it.
Centerline: Ball exactly on the center line — either player may strike; communicate to avoid paddle collisions.

name: Sniper-Pong (Dodgeball Variant)
tagline: Table-Tennis Tennis plus body targeting — dodge or lose.
rules: Players: 2, 4, or 6.
Baseline: Same as Table-Tennis Tennis — volleys allowed, direct serves.
Scoring — standard: Ball onto the table past the enemy.
Scoring — sniper: Deliberately strike the ball at an opponent's body; if it hits them before ground or wall, you score. In doubles/trios, optional rule: must hit the player who is due to return.
Risk/reward: Failed snipe that doesn't land legally on the table — your team loses the point.
Defensive paddles: Block a snipe; if it bounces back to the table, play continues. If it flies out after a block, the defender loses the point.
